=== PLAN ===
Build a browser-based Yew/Rust/WASM APL environment for COR24. Single-panel REPL with printer-style emulation, floating hardware panel (S2 switch, D2 LED, UART TX/RX), demo dropdown, file upload, and reset. Phase 1: scaffold Yew project with Trunk, wire cor24-emulator path dep. Phase 2: REPL panel (scrolling output, 6-space indent prompt, keyboard capture) + control bar. Phase 3: emulator integration (EmulatorBridge, UART I/O bridge, batch execution, pre-assemble APL interpreter via build.rs). Phase 4: hardware panel + demo registry + file upload. Phase 5: polish, responsive design, GitHub Pages deployment. Phase 6: APL glyph prettification display modes. Phase 7: New demos showcasing interpreter features (bracket indexing, bitwise ops, reverse/catenate, take/drop, error handling, control flow loops). Phase 8: Extend prettification to cover new keywords (rev, cat, and, or, not, goto). Phase 9: Command history with up/down arrow keys. Phase 10: Scroll-lock behavior (auto-scroll pauses when user scrolls up). Phase 11: APL quick-reference help overlay. Phase 12: Deploy all new features to GitHub Pages.

=== PROMPT ===
Phase 17: Design and implement an editor mode for APL programs.

Add an editor mode that allows the user to:
1. Edit a loaded Demo program before running it
2. Edit an uploaded file before running it
3. Create a new empty file and write APL code from scratch

UI design considerations:
- Add an 'Edit' button or mode toggle accessible from the control bar
- When a demo is selected or file uploaded, instead of immediately sending to REPL, open in editor
- Editor should be a textarea or contenteditable area with monospace font, Catppuccin Mocha styling
- Provide a 'Run' button to send the editor contents to the REPL via UART
- Provide a 'Download' link/button to save the editor contents as a .apl file
- Provide a 'New' button to clear the editor for a fresh program
- Editor should support the same APL glyph prettification display as the REPL (show glyphs in chosen display mode)
- Consider whether editor is a panel/tab alongside REPL or a modal overlay
- The editor contents should persist across demo switches (warn if unsaved changes)

Build, test, and verify the editor mode works end-to-end: load demo -> edit -> run -> see output in REPL. Also test: new file -> write code -> run -> download.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive report: ## Complete Web-SW-COR24-APL Project Exploration Report ### Project Overview This is a **Yew-based WebAssembly application** that provides a browser-based APL interpreter environment for the COR24 single-board computer. It emulates the COR24 processor in WASM, allowing users to write and execute APL code interactively via a web REPL. --- ### 1. File Structure ``` src/ ├── lib.rs # Main App component, Msg enum, message routing ├── repl.rs # ReplPanel component (UART TX/RX, emulator loop) ├── demos.rs # Demo registry (20+ pre-defined APL programs) ├── help.rs # HelpOverlay with tabbed interface (Quick Ref + Tutorial) ├── control_bar.rs # Control buttons, demo dropdown, file upload ├── prettify.rs # APL glyph/literate prettification & keyword translation ├── hardware.rs # HardwarePanel (LED, switch, UART byte display) ├── config.rs # Embedded APL binary reference ├── main.rs # WASM entry point ├── app.css # All styling (Catppuccin Mocha theme) └── (Catppuccin color vars in index.html) index.html # HTML skeleton + CSS variable definitions ``` --- ### 2. Architecture & Message Flow #### Top-Level App Component (lib.rs) **Msg enum:** ```rust pub enum Msg { Reset, // Clear workspace LoadDemo(String), // Load demo source text UploadProgram(String), // Load program from file ToggleS2, // Toggle S2 switch state HwState(bool, Option, Option), // Hardware state callback SetDisplayMode(DisplayMode), // Change prettification mode ToggleHelp, // Show/hide help overlay } ``` **App struct state:** ```rust pub struct App { reset_seq: u32, // Bumped to signal reset to ReplPanel feed_seq: u32, // Bumped when demo_text changes feed_text: AttrValue, // Text to feed to REPL s2_on: bool, // Hardware switch state led_on: bool, // D2 LED state (from emulator feedback) last_tx: Option, // Last UART byte transmitted last_rx: Option, // Last UART byte received display_mode: DisplayMode, // Repr | Glyph | Literate help_visible: bool, // Help overlay toggle } ``` **Component tree:** ``` App ├── Header (h1 "APL", span "COR24 Environment") ├── ControlBar │ ├── Reset button │ ├── Demo dropdown (DEMOS registry) │ ├── Upload file input │ ├── Display mode toggle (latin/greek/keywords) │ ├── Help button (?) │ └── IO indicator (⎕IO←0) ├── main#main-content │ ├── ReplPanel │ │ ├── Emulator (cor24_emulator::EmulatorCore) │ │ ├── Output buffer (Vec) │ │ ├── Input line (String) │ │ ├── UART RX queue (VecDeque) │ │ ├── Partial line buffer │ │ ├── .repl-output (scrollable) │ │ │ ├── Rendered lines with prettification │ │ │ ├── Current input line with cursor │ │ │ └── Scroll-to-bottom button (when user scrolls up) │ │ └── Tick loop (every 25ms: 50K instructions/batch) │ └── HardwarePanel │ ├── S2 switch display/clickable │ ├── D2 LED indicator │ └── TX/RX byte display (hex) └── HelpOverlay (conditional, tabbed) ├── Quick Reference tab (keyword table + syntax) └── Tutorial tab (18 sections with examples) ├── GitHub corner (link to repo) └── Footer (license, author, build info) ``` --- ### 3. REPL Component (repl.rs) — Core Logic **Msg enum:** ```rust pub enum Msg { KeyDown(KeyboardEvent), // User input Init, // Load APL binary Tick, // 25ms emulation loop Scroll, // Detect if user scrolled away from bottom ScrollToBottom, // Jump back to bottom } ``` **ReplPanel state:** ```rust pub struct ReplPanel { emulator: EmulatorCore, // COR24 emulator instance output: Vec, // All output lines input: String, // Current input line (being typed) running: bool, halted: bool, // Set by )OFF or halt uart_rx_queue: VecDeque, // Bytes waiting to send to emulator partial_line: String, // UART output fragment (no \n yet) _tick_handle: Option, // 25ms interval handle output_ref: NodeRef, // Ref to .repl-output div panel_ref: NodeRef, // Ref to .repl-panel (for focus) needs_focus: bool, last_reset_seq: u32, // Track parent's reset signal last_feed_seq: u32, // Track parent's feed signal last_tx: Option, // Report to parent last_rx: Option, // Report to parent history: Vec, // Command history history_index: Option, // Current position in history history_saved_input: String, // Input before history browse user_scrolled_up: bool, // Show scroll button? } ``` #### Key Methods: **`load_apl_binary()`** — Executed on Init or Reset - Loads pre-assembled APL binary from `config::APL_BINARY` - Calls `emulator.hard_reset()`, `load_program(0, binary)`, `set_pc(0)`, `resume()` - Clears output, UART queues **`feed_uart_bytes()`** — Called each tick ```rust // Check UART RX buffer ready (addr 0xFF0101, bit 0) // Pop from uart_rx_queue, send to emulator if ready // Track last_rx byte ``` **`collect_uart()`** — Called each tick ```rust // Get output from emulator.get_uart_output() // Split on '\n', accumulate in partial_line // Track last_tx byte // Push complete lines to output buffer ``` **`render_line(line, mode)`** — View helper ```rust // If line starts with " " (6-space PROMPT): // - Extract content after prompt // - Call prettify_line(content, mode) → Segments // - Render with keyword highlighting // Else: render as plain text ``` #### User Input Handling (Msg::KeyDown): ``` Enter → Echo (as " " + translated), queue to UART, save to history ArrowUp → Browse history backwards ArrowDown → Browse history forwards, restore saved input Backspace → Pop from current input [a-z0-9 ] → Append to input Ctrl/Cmd → Pass through to browser (copy/paste) ``` #### Tick Loop (25ms): 1. Feed any queued UART bytes to emulator 2. Run 50,000 instructions 3. Collect UART output 4. Check for halt (StopReason::Halted → set flag, stop ticking) 5. Report hardware state to parent: `(led_on, last_tx, last_rx)` 6. Schedule next tick if still running --- ### 4. Demos Registry (demos.rs) **Structure:** ```rust pub struct Demo { pub name: &'static str, pub description: &'static str, pub source: &'static str, // Multiline APL code } pub const DEMOS: &[Demo] = &[...20 demos...]; ``` **Demo examples:** - "Scalars" → `3 + 4`, `2 + 3 * 4`, etc. - "Vectors" → Vector literals and ops - "Matrices" → Reshape operations - "Control Flow" → Loop, labels, goto - "Hardware I/O" → qled, qsw, qsvo - "Error Handling" → Division by zero, undefined vars, etc. - "Multiline Programs" → [1] [2] [3] syntax, )RUN, )ERASE **How demos are sent:** 1. User selects from dropdown in ControlBar 2. ControlBar emits `on_demo(demo.source.to_string())` 3. App receives `Msg::LoadDemo(String)`, bumps `feed_seq`, stores `feed_text` 4. ReplPanel detects `feed_seq` change in `changed()`, calls `feed_program_text()` 5. Each line is trimmed, translated (literate→ASCII), then queued to UART --- ### 5. File Upload (control_bar.rs) **Flow:** 1. `` with `onchange={on_file_change}` 2. `on_file_change` → `Msg::FileChanged(File)` 3. `update()` calls `gloo::file::read_as_text()` with async callback 4. Callback → `Msg::FileLoaded(String)` 5. `on_upload.emit(text)` → App receives `Msg::UploadProgram(text)` 6. App sets `feed_text`, bumps `feed_seq` 7. Same flow as demos --- ### 6. UART TX/RX & Input Processing **UART TX (Emulator → Output):** - Every tick, collect UART output from emulator - Characters become lines or partial lines - Rendered with `.repl-line` and `.repl-prompt` (blue " ") **UART RX (User Input → Emulator):** - User types or demo loads - Text queued as bytes to `uart_rx_queue` - Each tick, `feed_uart_bytes()` checks emulator UART status (0xFF0101, bit 0) - If not busy (bit 0 = 0), pop bytes and send **Prettification Applied to Input:** - User's input line goes through `translate_literate_to_ascii()` before queuing - Echoed input also goes through `prettify_line()` for display --- ### 7. Control Bar (control_bar.rs) **Components:** ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ [Reset] [Demo ▼] [Upload .apl] [latin] [greek] [keywords] [?] ⎕IO←0 ⎕ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Buttons/Controls:** 1. **Reset** — Red button, calls `on_reset` → `Msg::Reset` (bumps reset_seq) 2. **Demo dropdown** — Selects from DEMOS, calls `on_demo(source)` → `Msg::LoadDemo` 3. **Upload .apl** — File input label, reads file, calls `on_upload` → `Msg::UploadProgram` 4. **Display mode group** (toggle buttons): - `latin` (Repr) — Show raw ASCII keywords - `greek` (Glyph) — Show APL Unicode glyphs - `keywords` (Literate) — Show English names - Active button highlighted with `.btn-mode-active` (mauve bg) 5. **Help button (?)** — Calls `on_help` → `Msg::ToggleHelp` 6. **IO indicator** — Shows `⎕IO←0` (always visible, right side) --- ### 8. Help Overlay (help.rs) **Structure:** ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ [Quick Reference] [Tutorial] Esc to close │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Tab 1: Quick Reference │ │ - Intro paragraph (latin/greek/keywords modes) │ │ - Keyword table (4 columns: Type, APL, Meaning, Alias) │ - Syntax sections (Arithmetic, Variables, Output, etc.) │ │ │ Tab 2: Tutorial │ │ - 18 numbered sections (Scalars → Bitwise) │ │ - Each has description + examples │ │ - Example shows input/output side-by-side │ │ - Optional glyph variant below ASCII │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Msg enum:** ```rust pub enum Msg { KeyDown(KeyboardEvent), // Esc to close BackdropClick(MouseEvent), // Click outside closes SwitchTab(Tab), // Click tab header } ``` **Tab enum:** ```rust pub enum Tab { QuickReference, Tutorial, } ``` **Key features:** - Backdrop click closes (but only if clicking outside dialog) - Esc key closes - Quick Reference uses `.help-table` with keyword rows - Tutorial sections use `.tut-section` with examples - Prettification applied to example input for glyph display --- ### 9. Prettification System (prettify.rs) **DisplayMode enum:** ```rust #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Default)] pub enum DisplayMode { #[default] Repr, // Show raw ASCII: "rho", "iota" Glyph, // Show Unicode: "⍴", "⍳", "↑", "↓", etc. Literate, // Show English: "shape-of", "index-gen", etc. } ``` **Segment enum:** ```rust pub enum Segment { Plain(String), // Not a keyword Keyword(String), // Prettified keyword (for CSS highlighting) } ``` **Keyword Table (13 entries):** ``` ascii | glyph | literate_monadic | literate_dyadic ---------|--------|------------------|---------------- rho | ⍴ | shape-of | reshape iota | ⍳ | index-gen | (none) take | ↑ | take | (none) drop | ↓ | drop | (none) rev | ⌽ | reverse | (none) cat | , | ravel | catenate and | ∧ | and | (none) or | ∨ | or | (none) not | ∼ | complement | (none) qled | ⎕LED | LED | (none) qsw | ⎕SW | switch | (none) qsvo | ⎕SVO | share | share goto | → | branch | (none) ``` **`prettify_line(line, mode) → Vec`:** - Scans for keyword matches at word boundaries (lowercase alpha, not alphanumeric before/after) - In Repr mode: returns single Plain segment - In Glyph/Literate mode: replaces keywords with glyphs/names - Detects dyadic usage (literate) by checking if non-whitespace precedes keyword **`translate_literate_to_ascii(input) → String`:** - Reverse mapping: "shape-of" → "rho", "reshape" → "rho", etc. - Longest-match-first (avoid partial matches) - Used when processing user input or demo text before sending to UART --- ### 10. CSS Styling (app.css + index.html) #### Design System (Catppuccin Mocha): ```css /* Base colors */ --crust: #11111b; /* Darkest background */ --mantle: #181825; /* Control bar bg */ --base: #1e1e2e; /* REPL background */ --surface0/1/2: #313244, #45475a, #585b70; --text: #cdd6f4; /* Primary text */ /* Semantic colors */ --blue: #89b4fa; /* Prompt, help, links *) --green: #a6e3a1; /* REPL output, input, keywords in help *) --mauve: #cba6f7; /* Keywords (APL), active buttons *) --red: #f38ba8; /* Reset button *) --yellow: #f9e2af; /* Help button *) --teal: #94e2d5; /* IO indicator *) ``` #### Layout Components: **Header:** ```css header { padding: 14px 20px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--surface1); } header h1 { font-size: 22px; color: var(--blue); } ``` **Control Bar:** ```css .control-bar { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--surface1); background: var(--mantle); } .btn { font-family: var(--mono); font-size: 13px; padding: 4px 12px; border: 1px solid var(--surface2); border-radius: 4px; ... } .btn-reset { color: var(--red); border-color: var(--red); } .btn-upload { color: var(--blue); border-color: var(--blue); } .btn-help { color: var(--yellow); border-color: var(--yellow); } .mode-group { display: inline-flex; gap: 0; margin-left: auto; } .btn-mode { border-radius: 0; border-right-width: 0; } .btn-mode-active { background: var(--mauve); color: var(--base); } ``` **REPL Panel:** ```css .repl-panel { flex: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; outline: none; } .repl-output { flex: 1; overflow-y: auto; padding: 12px 16px; background: var(--base); font-family: var(--mono); font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.5; color: var(--green); white-space: pre-wrap; word-break: break-all; } .repl-prompt { color: var(--blue); } .repl-input-text { color: var(--text); } .repl-cursor { animation: cursor-blink 1.06s step-end infinite; } .scroll-to-bottom { position: absolute; bottom: 12px; left: 16px; background: rgba(..., 0.9); border-radius: 4px; } ``` **Hardware Panel:** ```css .hw-panel { position: absolute; bottom: 12px; right: 16px; background: rgba(24, 24, 37, 0.9); border-radius: 6px; padding: 10px 14px; font-size: 12px; } .hw-led { width: 12px; height: 12px; border-radius: 50%; } .hw-led-on { background: var(--green); box-shadow: 0 0 6px var(--green); } .hw-led-off { background: var(--surface0); border: 1px solid var(--surface1); } .hw-switch { padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 3px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600; } .hw-switch-on { background: var(--blue); color: var(--base); } .hw-switch-off { background: var(--surface0); } ``` **Help Overlay:** ```css .help-backdrop { position: fixed; inset: 0; background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.6); z-index: 100; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; } .help-dialog { background: var(--surface0); Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive report: ## Complete Web-SW-COR24-APL Project Exploration Report ### Project Overview This is a **Yew-based WebAssembly application** that provides a browser-based APL interpreter environment for the COR24 single-board computer. It emulates the COR24 processor in WASM, allowing users to write and execute APL code interactively via a web REPL. --- ### 1. File Structure ``` src/ ├── lib.rs # Main App component, Msg enum, message routing ├── repl.rs # ReplPanel component (UART TX/RX, emulator loop) ├── demos.rs # Demo registry (20+ pre-defined APL programs) ├── help.rs # HelpOverlay with tabbed interface (Quick Ref + Tutorial) ├── control_bar.rs # Control buttons, demo dropdown, file upload ├── prettify.rs # APL glyph/literate prettification & keyword translation ├── hardware.rs # HardwarePanel (LED, switch, UART byte display) ├── config.rs # Embedded APL binary reference ├── main.rs # WASM entry point ├── app.css # All styling (Catppuccin Mocha theme) └── (Catppuccin color vars in index.html) index.html # HTML skeleton + CSS variable definitions ``` --- ### 2. Architecture & Message Flow #### Top-Level App Component (lib.rs) **Msg enum:** ```rust pub enum Msg { Reset, // Clear workspace LoadDemo(String), // Load demo source text UploadProgram(String), // Load program from file ToggleS2, // Toggle S2 switch state HwState(bool, Option, Option), // Hardware state callback SetDisplayMode(DisplayMode), // Change prettification mode ToggleHelp, // Show/hide help overlay } ``` **App struct state:** ```rust pub struct App { reset_seq: u32, // Bumped to signal reset to ReplPanel feed_seq: u32, // Bumped when demo_text changes feed_text: AttrValue, // Text to feed to REPL s2_on: bool, // Hardware switch state led_on: bool, // D2 LED state (from emulator feedback) last_tx: Option, // Last UART byte transmitted last_rx: Option, // Last UART byte received display_mode: DisplayMode, // Repr | Glyph | Literate help_visible: bool, // Help overlay toggle } ``` **Component tree:** ``` App ├── Header (h1 "APL", span "COR24 Environment") ├── ControlBar │ ├── Reset button │ ├── Demo dropdown (DEMOS registry) │ ├── Upload file input │ ├── Display mode toggle (latin/greek/keywords) │ ├── Help button (?) │ └── IO indicator (⎕IO←0) ├── main#main-content │ ├── ReplPanel │ │ ├── Emulator (cor24_emulator::EmulatorCore) │ │ ├── Output buffer (Vec) │ │ ├── Input line (String) │ │ ├── UART RX queue (VecDeque) │ │ ├── Partial line buffer │ │ ├── .repl-output (scrollable) │ │ │ ├── Rendered lines with prettification │ │ │ ├── Current input line with cursor │ │ │ └── Scroll-to-bottom button (when user scrolls up) │ │ └── Tick loop (every 25ms: 50K instructions/batch) │ └── HardwarePanel │ ├── S2 switch display/clickable │ ├── D2 LED indicator │ └── TX/RX byte display (hex) └── HelpOverlay (conditional, tabbed) ├── Quick Reference tab (keyword table + syntax) └── Tutorial tab (18 sections with examples) ├── GitHub corner (link to repo) └── Footer (license, author, build info) ``` --- ### 3. REPL Component (repl.rs) — Core Logic **Msg enum:** ```rust pub enum Msg { KeyDown(KeyboardEvent), // User input Init, // Load APL binary Tick, // 25ms emulation loop Scroll, // Detect if user scrolled away from bottom ScrollToBottom, // Jump back to bottom } ``` **ReplPanel state:** ```rust pub struct ReplPanel { emulator: EmulatorCore, // COR24 emulator instance output: Vec, // All output lines input: String, // Current input line (being typed) running: bool, halted: bool, // Set by )OFF or halt uart_rx_queue: VecDeque, // Bytes waiting to send to emulator partial_line: String, // UART output fragment (no \n yet) _tick_handle: Option, // 25ms interval handle output_ref: NodeRef, // Ref to .repl-output div panel_ref: NodeRef, // Ref to .repl-panel (for focus) needs_focus: bool, last_reset_seq: u32, // Track parent's reset signal last_feed_seq: u32, // Track parent's feed signal last_tx: Option, // Report to parent last_rx: Option, // Report to parent history: Vec, // Command history history_index: Option, // Current position in history history_saved_input: String, // Input before history browse user_scrolled_up: bool, // Show scroll button? } ``` #### Key Methods: **`load_apl_binary()`** — Executed on Init or Reset - Loads pre-assembled APL binary from `config::APL_BINARY` - Calls `emulator.hard_reset()`, `load_program(0, binary)`, `set_pc(0)`, `resume()` - Clears output, UART queues **`feed_uart_bytes()`** — Called each tick ```rust // Check UART RX buffer ready (addr 0xFF0101, bit 0) // Pop from uart_rx_queue, send to emulator if ready // Track last_rx byte ``` **`collect_uart()`** — Called each tick ```rust // Get output from emulator.get_uart_output() // Split on '\n', accumulate in partial_line // Track last_tx byte // Push complete lines to output buffer ``` **`render_line(line, mode)`** — View helper ```rust // If line starts with " " (6-space PROMPT): // - Extract content after prompt // - Call prettify_line(content, mode) → Segments // - Render with keyword highlighting // Else: render as plain text ``` #### User Input Handling (Msg::KeyDown): ``` Enter → Echo (as " " + translated), queue to UART, save to history ArrowUp → Browse history backwards ArrowDown → Browse history forwards, restore saved input Backspace → Pop from current input [a-z0-9 ] → Append to input Ctrl/Cmd → Pass through to browser (copy/paste) ``` #### Tick Loop (25ms): 1. Feed any queued UART bytes to emulator 2. Run 50,000 instructions 3. Collect UART output 4. Check for halt (StopReason::Halted → set flag, stop ticking) 5. Report hardware state to parent: `(led_on, last_tx, last_rx)` 6. Schedule next tick if still running --- ### 4. Demos Registry (demos.rs) **Structure:** ```rust pub struct Demo { pub name: &'static str, pub description: &'static str, pub source: &'static str, // Multiline APL code } pub const DEMOS: &[Demo] = &[...20 demos...]; ``` **Demo examples:** - "Scalars" → `3 + 4`, `2 + 3 * 4`, etc. - "Vectors" → Vector literals and ops - "Matrices" → Reshape operations - "Control Flow" → Loop, labels, goto - "Hardware I/O" → qled, qsw, qsvo - "Error Handling" → Division by zero, undefined vars, etc. - "Multiline Programs" → [1] [2] [3] syntax, )RUN, )ERASE **How demos are sent:** 1. User selects from dropdown in ControlBar 2. ControlBar emits `on_demo(demo.source.to_string())` 3. App receives `Msg::LoadDemo(String)`, bumps `feed_seq`, stores `feed_text` 4. ReplPanel detects `feed_seq` change in `changed()`, calls `feed_program_text()` 5. Each line is trimmed, translated (literate→ASCII), then queued to UART --- ### 5. File Upload (control_bar.rs) **Flow:** 1. `` with `onchange={on_file_change}` 2. `on_file_change` → `Msg::FileChanged(File)` 3. `update()` calls `gloo::file::read_as_text()` with async callback 4. Callback → `Msg::FileLoaded(String)` 5. `on_upload.emit(text)` → App receives `Msg::UploadProgram(text)` 6. App sets `feed_text`, bumps `feed_seq` 7. Same flow as demos --- ### 6. UART TX/RX & Input Processing **UART TX (Emulator → Output):** - Every tick, collect UART output from emulator - Characters become lines or partial lines - Rendered with `.repl-line` and `.repl-prompt` (blue " ") **UART RX (User Input → Emulator):** - User types or demo loads - Text queued as bytes to `uart_rx_queue` - Each tick, `feed_uart_bytes()` checks emulator UART status (0xFF0101, bit 0) - If not busy (bit 0 = 0), pop bytes and send **Prettification Applied to Input:** - User's input line goes through `translate_literate_to_ascii()` before queuing - Echoed input also goes through `prettify_line()` for display --- ### 7. Control Bar (control_bar.rs) **Components:** ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ [Reset] [Demo ▼] [Upload .apl] [latin] [greek] [keywords] [?] ⎕IO←0 ⎕ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Buttons/Controls:** 1. **Reset** — Red button, calls `on_reset` → `Msg::Reset` (bumps reset_seq) 2. **Demo dropdown** — Selects from DEMOS, calls `on_demo(source)` → `Msg::LoadDemo` 3. **Upload .apl** — File input label, reads file, calls `on_upload` → `Msg::UploadProgram` 4. **Display mode group** (toggle buttons): - `latin` (Repr) — Show raw ASCII keywords - `greek` (Glyph) — Show APL Unicode glyphs - `keywords` (Literate) — Show English names - Active button highlighted with `.btn-mode-active` (mauve bg) 5. **Help button (?)** — Calls `on_help` → `Msg::ToggleHelp` 6. **IO indicator** — Shows `⎕IO←0` (always visible, right side) --- ### 8. Help Overlay (help.rs) **Structure:** ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ [Quick Reference] [Tutorial] Esc to close │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Tab 1: Quick Reference │ │ - Intro paragraph (latin/greek/keywords modes) │ │ - Keyword table (4 columns: Type, APL, Meaning, Alias) │ - Syntax sections (Arithmetic, Variables, Output, etc.) │ │ │ Tab 2: Tutorial │ │ - 18 numbered sections (Scalars → Bitwise) │ │ - Each has description + examples │ │ - Example shows input/output side-by-side │ │ - Optional glyph variant below ASCII │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` **Msg enum:** ```rust pub enum Msg { KeyDown(KeyboardEvent), // Esc to close BackdropClick(MouseEvent), // Click outside closes SwitchTab(Tab), // Click tab header } ``` **Tab enum:** ```rust pub enum Tab { QuickReference, Tutorial, } ``` **Key features:** - Backdrop click closes (but only if clicking outside dialog) - Esc key closes - Quick Reference uses `.help-table` with keyword rows - Tutorial sections use `.tut-section` with examples - Prettification applied to example input for glyph display --- ### 9. Prettification System (prettify.rs) **DisplayMode enum:** ```rust #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Default)] pub enum DisplayMode { #[default] Repr, // Show raw ASCII: "rho", "iota" Glyph, // Show Unicode: "⍴", "⍳", "↑", "↓", etc. Literate, // Show English: "shape-of", "index-gen", etc. } ``` **Segment enum:** ```rust pub enum Segment { Plain(String), // Not a keyword Keyword(String), // Prettified keyword (for CSS highlighting) } ``` **Keyword Table (13 entries):** ``` ascii | glyph | literate_monadic | literate_dyadic ---------|--------|------------------|---------------- rho | ⍴ | shape-of | reshape iota | ⍳ | index-gen | (none) take | ↑ | take | (none) drop | ↓ | drop | (none) rev | ⌽ | reverse | (none) cat | , | ravel | catenate and | ∧ | and | (none) or | ∨ | or | (none) not | ∼ | complement | (none) qled | ⎕LED | LED | (none) qsw | ⎕SW | switch | (none) qsvo | ⎕SVO | share | share goto | → | branch | (none) ``` **`prettify_line(line, mode) → Vec`:** - Scans for keyword matches at word boundaries (lowercase alpha, not alphanumeric before/after) - In Repr mode: returns single Plain segment - In Glyph/Literate mode: replaces keywords with glyphs/names - Detects dyadic usage (literate) by checking if non-whitespace precedes keyword **`translate_literate_to_ascii(input) → String`:** - Reverse mapping: "shape-of" → "rho", "reshape" → "rho", etc. - Longest-match-first (avoid partial matches) - Used when processing user input or demo text before sending to UART --- ### 10. CSS Styling (app.css + index.html) #### Design System (Catppuccin Mocha): ```css /* Base colors */ --crust: #11111b; /* Darkest background */ --mantle: #181825; /* Control bar bg */ --base: #1e1e2e; /* REPL background */ --surface0/1/2: #313244, #45475a, #585b70; --text: #cdd6f4; /* Primary text */ /* Semantic colors */ --blue: #89b4fa; /* Prompt, help, links *) --green: #a6e3a1; /* REPL output, input, keywords in help *) --mauve: #cba6f7; /* Keywords (APL), active buttons *) --red: #f38ba8; /* Reset button *) --yellow: #f9e2af; /* Help button *) --teal: #94e2d5; /* IO indicator *) ``` #### Layout Components: **Header:** ```css header { padding: 14px 20px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--surface1); } header h1 { font-size: 22px; color: var(--blue); } ``` **Control Bar:** ```css .control-bar { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 8px 16px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--surface1); background: var(--mantle); } .btn { font-family: var(--mono); font-size: 13px; padding: 4px 12px; border: 1px solid var(--surface2); border-radius: 4px; ... } .btn-reset { color: var(--red); border-color: var(--red); } .btn-upload { color: var(--blue); border-color: var(--blue); } .btn-help { color: var(--yellow); border-color: var(--yellow); } .mode-group { display: inline-flex; gap: 0; margin-left: auto; } .btn-mode { border-radius: 0; border-right-width: 0; } .btn-mode-active { background: var(--mauve); color: var(--base); } ``` **REPL Panel:** ```css .repl-panel { flex: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; outline: none; } .repl-output { flex: 1; # /mw-cp — Mike Wright's Checkpoint Process

You are executing a disciplined checkpoint commit. Follow every phase in order.
Do NOT skip any phase, even if it seems clean. Report status after each phase.

---

## Phase 1 — Pre-Commit Checks

Run all of the following that apply to this project. Stop and report failures before proceeding.

1. **Format**: `cargo fmt --check` (or `rustfmt --check` on specific files)
2. **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
3. **Build**: `cargo build` (debug is fine; surface any compile errors)
4. **Tests**: `cargo test` — all tests must pass
5. **Unused deps** (if `cargo-machete` present): `cargo machete`
6. **Project-local checks**: run `just check` or `just pre-commit` if a justfile exists

If any check fails, STOP. Report what failed and ask the user how to proceed.

---

## Phase 2 — Documentation Updates

Review recent changes and update the following as appropriate:

1. **CHANGELOG.md / CHANGES.md** — add an entry under `[Unreleased]` or today's date
2. **STATUS.md** — update current state, what changed, what's in progress
3. **LEARNINGS.md** — capture anything notable: decisions made, gotchas, tradeoffs
4. **Inline docs** — ensure any new public API items have doc comments (`///`)
5. **README.md** — update if any user-facing behavior, config, or setup changed

If none of these files exist, note that and skip gracefully.

---

## Phase 3 — Staged Diff Review

Run `git diff --staged` (or `git diff HEAD` if nothing staged yet).

Summarize what is changing:
- Files modified, added, deleted
- High-level functional description of the change

Then stage all changes: `git add -A`

---

## Phase 4 — Commit

Compose a detailed commit message following Conventional Commits style:
```
<type>(<scope>): <short imperative summary>

<body — explain WHAT changed and WHY, not HOW>
<mention any tradeoffs, alternatives considered, or follow-ups needed>

Refs: <issue/ticket numbers if applicable>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `perf`, `build`

If  was provided, incorporate it as context for the message.

Execute: `git commit -m "<message>"`

---

## Phase 5 — Push

1. Check current branch: `git branch --show-current`
2. Check remote: `git remote -v`
3. Push: `git push origin <branch>`

If the push is rejected (non-fast-forward), STOP and report — do NOT force push without explicit user confirmation.

---

## Phase 6 — Checkpoint Summary

Report a brief summary:
- ✅ Checks passed
- 📝 Docs updated (list files)
- 🔖 Commit hash and message
- 🚀 Pushed to remote/branch

Flag any items that were skipped or need follow-up.
```
